text: The website shall provide information about careers in computer science to Oregon high school students.
The website shall include personal and career information from a broad spectrum of CS professionals.
The website shall provide CS course and other information from OUS campuses.
The website shall include a call to action and plans for students to follow to prepare for a CS career.
The website shall adhere to CSS and style conventions established in Get Real version 1.
The website shall provide a listing of computer science offerings from Oregon University System campuses and other Oregon colleges.
The website shall provide answers to questions regarding societal contributions and potential earnings with a computer science degree.
The website shall include a Real People section featuring profiles of recent CS graduates and experienced professionals.
The website shall profile women computer scientists and engineers in the Women in Computing section.
The website shall provide a list of pre-college technology programs and courses for high school students.
The website shall include an 'I'm Looking For...' section with focused questions and links regarding money, people, and colleges.
The website shall display a list of external articles related to computer science careers.
The website shall provide recommended high school curricula for students interested in CS.
The website shall include an internal search function to search the Get Real site.
The website shall provide an external search function for outside CS related sites.
The website shall provide a listing with links to professional organizations including ACM and IEEE.
The website shall provide RSS feeds of current CS information from the chancellor's office.
The website shall include an FAQ section for questions asked to graduates or professors.
The website shall provide information on the value of a CS minor for other professions.
The website shall be compatible with and tested against IE, Firefox, and Netscape.
The website shall be hosted on a server that provides response times comparable to the current OUS site.
The website shall implement the same copyright and security measures as the OUS site.
The website shall track and evaluate user hits and time spent on the site.
The website shall utilize design conventions consistent with OUS site standards to accommodate short reader attention spans.
